result.summary: Authzed exposes a documented SpiceDB HTTP API, two MCP servers (a remote AuthZed docs/examples server and a local SpiceDB Dev MCP server), and the `zed` CLI; the CLI uses SpiceDB/AuthZed tokens, while HTTP/MCP auth details are only partially documented in the pages found.
result.description: AuthZed provides authorization infrastructure for modern applications and develops SpiceDB, an open source permissions database inspired by Google's Zanzibar. Its products let teams model, store, and query fine-grained permissions for applications and AI systems.

zed_token.label: SpiceDB/AuthZed access token
zed_token.setup: Create or obtain a SpiceDB/AuthZed token for your cluster, then configure it in a Zed context with `zed context set <name> <endpoint> <token>` or pass it directly with `--token` / `ZED_TOKEN`. The Zed README shows example tokens and headless usage; the docs nav links to [Getting Started with Authzed Cloud](https://authzed.com/docs/authzed/guides/cloud) for managed deployments and the CLI setup page at [Installing Zed](https://authzed.com/docs/spicedb/getting-started/installing-zed).
